articles.title: Tesla wins first European approval for FSD supervised (TSLA:NASDAQ)
articles.summary: Tesla has received its first European approval for its Full Self-Driving (FSD) supervised software in the Netherlands. This approval allows the use of FSD with mandatory human supervision on both highways and city streets, a significant step for Tesla's expansion of the technology within the European Union.
articles.text_to_analyze: Tesla wins first European approval for FSD supervised (TSLA:NASDAQ). articles.title: Tesla Bars Customers From Selling Model S, X 'Signature' Edition Within One Year - Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA)
articles.summary: Tesla has implemented a clause in its purchase agreement for Model S and Model X 'Signature' edition customers, prohibiting them from selling their vehicles within one year of delivery due to the limited release nature. If unforeseen circumstances necessitate a sale, Tesla reserves the right to repurchase the vehicle at a depreciated price. Additionally, the article notes poor momentum and value for TSLA according to Benzinga Edge Rankings, despite a recent surge in after-hours trading.

articles.title: How Graniteshares 1.25x Long Tsla Daily Etf (TSL) Affects Rotational Strategy Timing
articles.summary: This article analyzes the Graniteshares 1.25x Long Tsla Daily Etf (TSL), highlighting weak near and mid-term sentiment despite a long-term positive outlook. It provides institutional trading strategies including a position strategy, momentum breakout strategy, and risk hedging strategy, alongside multi-timeframe signal analysis for support and resistance levels. The report emphasizes elevated downside risk due to a lack of additional long-term support signals.

articles.title: Q4 Earnings Highlights: Autoliv (NYSE:ALV) Vs The Rest Of The Automobile Manufacturing Stocks
articles.summary: Autoliv (NYSE:ALV) reported Q4 revenues of $2.82 billion, beating analyst expectations, but the stock is down 12.8% since. The article analyzes Autoliv's performance against other automobile manufacturing companies like Rivian, Lucid, Winnebago, and Tesla, and discusses trends in the sector such as the impact of EVs and geopolitical risks on the market. Overall, the automobile manufacturing sector saw mixed Q4 results, with revenues beating estimates by 3.8% but share prices declining by an average of 13.6% post-earnings.

articles.title: Weekend Tech Roundup: Pony AI's Upgrade, Tesla's Sales Dip, Waymo's Pothole Fix, Kia's Atlas Robot And GM
articles.summary: This weekend tech roundup covers several significant developments: Pony AI introduced an AI upgrade and expanded its robotaxi services, while Tesla experienced a 16.2% dip in China retail sales in Q1. Waymo partnered with Waze to help cities identify and fix potholes, and Kia announced plans to deploy Boston Dynamics' Atlas robot at its Georgia manufacturing plant. Additionally, General Motors initiated a recall of 271,000 Chevrolet Malibu vehicles due to a rearview camera issue.

articles.title: ‘Stay on the Sidelines,’ Says Top Investor About Tesla Stock
articles.summary: Tesla's recent performance has led top investor Daniel Sparks to advise staying on the sidelines regarding its stock. The company experienced a year-over-year decrease in sales for 2025 and a sequential drop in Q1 2026 deliveries and energy business. While Tesla is focusing on future AI initiatives, these ambitious projects will require significant capital, which could be challenged by weak performance in its core automotive and energy sectors.

articles.title: Barclays Bank PLC (ATMP) launches contingent‑coupon notes tied to NFLX, PLTR, TSLA
articles.summary: Barclays Bank PLC has launched contingent-coupon notes (ATMP) linked to the performance of Netflix (NFLX), Palantir (PLTR), and Tesla (TSLA) stocks. These notes offer a monthly contingent coupon of $7.083 per $1,000 (8.50% annualized) if the closing value of each underlying stock is at or above 75% of its initial value on observation dates. The notes also feature an automatic redemption mechanism where principal plus the contingent coupon is paid if all underlying stocks are at or above their initial value on observation dates, starting from the twelfth observation date.

articles.title: Roundhill TSLA WeeklyPay ETF announces weekly distribution of $0.1984
articles.summary: The Roundhill TSLA WeeklyPay ETF (NYSE Arca: TSLA) has announced a weekly distribution of $0.1984 per share. Shareowners as of April 15, 2024, will receive this distribution on or about April 22, 2024. This payment reflects the ETF's strategy to provide investors with weekly income.

articles.title: Ross Gerber Demands $10K Refund If Tesla's New FSD Doesn't Work 'Unsupervised'—Says Google Has 'Solved' Autonomy With Waymo
articles.summary: Investor Ross Gerber stated that Tesla Inc. should refund customers $10,000 if its new Full Self-Driving (FSD) v14.3 update doesn't work unsupervised, expressing that customers have waited long enough. He also praised Google's Waymo, claiming it has "solved" autonomy and drives better than humans. Meanwhile, Elon Musk continues to defend FSD, and the NHTSA's investigation into the system is ongoing.

articles.title: Tesla's self-driving software gets Dutch go-ahead, in boost for EU ambitions
articles.summary: Tesla's driver assistance software, which handles most driving tasks on highways and city streets under human supervision, has received regulatory approval from the Dutch vehicle authority, marking its first approval in Europe. This decision could facilitate the software's acceptance by other European authorities. The full self-driving software is already available in the U.S.

articles.title: Morgan Stanley Has Mostly Positive Outlook On Tesla FSD V15
articles.summary: Morgan Stanley released a research note on Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) Version 15, expressing a mostly positive outlook despite some caveats. The firm believes the upcoming FSD V15, with expanded model parameters and improved data collection, will be crucial for unlocking unsupervised autonomy and supporting the stock's valuation, especially as Tesla approaches 10 billion FSD miles. While acknowledging a slower-than-expected Robotaxi rollout, Morgan Stanley remains optimistic that Tesla will demonstrate progress in scaling unsupervised autonomous driving capabilities throughout 2026.
